problem.title_line1: Your brain struggles to write and
problem.subtitle: Lectures move fast. Notes fall behind. You end up choosing between understanding and capturing — and either way, something gets lost.

items.title: Hand cramps, missed words
items.desc: Writing fast enough to keep up means your notes are incomplete — or illegible.

items.title: ADHD makes it harder
items.desc: Splitting focus between listening and writing is exhausting. Something always slips.

items.title: Non-native language
items.desc: Following a fast lecture in your second language is double the cognitive load.

items.title: No notes = no studying
items.desc: Without written notes, reviewing means watching the whole lecture again.

origin.lede: We're Victoria and Pierre — a couple from the south of France. Victoria is studying anthropology online — every lecture, every seminar, every workshop streamed from home. Pierre loves building things with code. This started as a side project to see if a little Chrome extension could help — and turned into something we think everyone deserves.
origin.p1: Victoria's days are hours of video lectures back to back. Taking notes while watching meant losing the thread. Rewinding, rewriting, rewatching. Notes too sparse to study from, or too rushed to read. On top of that, she has ADHD — which makes splitting focus between listening and writing not just hard, but exhausting.
origin.p2: So Pierre started building, and Victoria tested every version — broke things, gave feedback, explained what was actually missing. Evening after evening, laptop to laptop.
origin.close: This is the result. We're making it available to everyone because no student should have to choose between listening and writing. If it helps you even once, it was worth it.

who.title: Built for people who learn by reading along

items.title: Students
items.desc: University lectures, online courses, MOOCs — capture everything without losing the thread of what's being said.

items.title: ADHD Learners
items.desc: Reading along while listening dramatically reduces cognitive load. Stop losing focus mid-lecture.

items.title: Non-native speakers
items.desc: Watching lectures in a second language? Having the transcript appear in real-time lets you follow along without missing a word.

items.title: Online Course Takers
items.desc: Udemy, Coursera, YouTube tutorials — turn any video into searchable, editable notes automatically.

items.title: Accuracy you can trust
items.desc: Audio is sent in chunks — 10 seconds in Normal mode, 30 seconds in Optimal for even better accuracy. A short delay, but far more accurate text than any real-time alternative.

items.title: A real note editor
items.desc: Don't just read — work directly in the transcript. Highlight key phrases, bold terms, add headers and bullets, remove the parts you don't need. Most students finish their notes before the lecture ends.

items.title: Export anywhere
items.desc: Download as .txt or .md, or copy to clipboard. Your notes, your format.

items.title: 14 languages
items.desc: English, French, Spanish, Arabic, Hindi and more. Transcribe in the language of your lecture — great for non-native speakers following along.

items.title: Privacy first
items.desc: Everything stays local by default — no account, no cloud processing in free mode. Your audio, your data.

items.title: Session history
items.desc: All your past transcriptions are saved automatically. Pick up where you left off — nothing is ever lost.

worksEverywhere.subtitle: University internal platforms, protected videos, public sites — if audio comes out of your computer, we can transcribe it.

platforms.title: University platforms
platforms.desc: Moodle, Canvas, Blackboard, Panopto — the ones with no download, no subtitles, and no help. If the video plays in your browser, we transcribe it.

platforms.title: Public video sites
platforms.desc: YouTube, Coursera, Udemy — all the classics. But also any embedded player on any site, no matter how obscure or non-standard.

platforms.title: Works with headphones
platforms.desc: AI mode captures tab audio directly from the browser — no microphone, no speakers needed. Study at 2am without disturbing anyone.

worksEverywhere.explainerEyebrow: How the two modes capture audio
worksEverywhere.explainerFree: Free mode uses your microphone to pick up audio through your speakers. Works on every platform — but volume matters. Turn system volume up for best accuracy.
worksEverywhere.explainerAI: AI mode captures the browser tab audio directly, bypassing your mic entirely. Clean capture with headphones on, at any volume, on any protected platform.

pricing.subtitle: Start for free with your microphone. Top up when you need AI-powered transcription — no subscription, no expiry.
pricing.whyPay: Why does AI mode cost money?
pricing.whyPayDesc: Free mode uses your browser's built-in speech recognition — local, instant, free. AI mode sends your audio to a state-of-the-art cloud AI model. That processing has a real cost. We pass it on transparently: credits are consumed second-by-second from your pack, they never expire, and you choose how fast you burn them based on the transcription mode you use.
pricing.freeDesc: Uses your browser's built-in speech engine and your microphone. Fully local — no audio sent anywhere.
freeFeatures: Full editing — highlights, bold, headers, bullets
pricing.freeCaveat: Audio must play through your speakers — the mic needs to hear it.
pricing.premiumLabel: AI Mode — Pay as you go
pricing.noSubCallout: No subscription. No "I forgot to cancel" tax. Credits never expire.
pricing.costCallout: Buy a pack once. Your hours are consumed second-by-second as you transcribe, so nothing is wasted when you stop. The bigger the pack, the lower the price per hour.
pricing.headphonesCallout: Works with headphones on. Tab audio is captured directly — no microphone, no speakers needed.
premiumFeatures: Everything in Free, including full editing
Cloud AI engine — far better accuracy than browser speech
Direct tab audio — works with any headphones
Higher accuracy across accents & background noise
Hours consumed second-by-second — nothing wasted when you stop
Credits never expire — top up whenever you want
10-min grace period if credits run out mid-lecture
pricing.currencyDisclaimer: Prices shown are estimates. Final amount is converted at the live exchange rate by Lemon Squeezy at checkout.
pricing.paymentProcessor: Payments processed securely by Lemon Squeezy.
pricing.footnote: Buy once, use whenever. A full semester at 3h/week costs around $21 — less than two textbook chapters.

items.q: How do credits work?
items.a: Buy a credit pack and you'll receive a license key by email. Open the extension settings, enter the key, and your credits activate immediately. They're consumed second-by-second as you transcribe — nothing is wasted when you stop.

items.q: I lost my activation code. What do I do?
items.a: Check your inbox — the key was sent when you purchased. Your key is portable: it works on any computer, survives reinstalls, and your remaining credits are always preserved. Think of it as a prepaid balance, not a device licence.

items.q: My transcription is in the wrong language.
items.a: Make sure you've selected the correct language in the extension settings before starting. If you changed language mid-session, stop the transcription, select the right language, and start again. The speech engine needs a clean restart to switch languages.

items.q: The transcription isn't working at all.
items.a: Try these steps in order: (1) Make sure the extension is up to date — check for updates in chrome://extensions. (2) Reload the extension by toggling it off and on. (3) Reload the page with the video. (4) Check your internet connection — AI mode requires it. (5) If you're using mic mode, make sure your system volume is high enough (80%+) and that you've granted the extension permission to access your microphone.

items.q: Microphone mode isn't picking up the audio.
items.a: This is almost always a volume issue. Free mode relies on your mic hearing your speakers — the louder your system volume, the better the pickup. MacBook Air users typically need a higher volume than MacBook Pro users. Also make sure you've allowed microphone access for the extension.

items.q: Can I transcribe multiple videos at the same time?
items.a: Yes — but only with AI mode. AI mode captures tab audio per tab, so each video runs independently. Free (mic) mode captures whatever your speakers play, so mixing audio sources will confuse the transcription.

youtube.h1: Transcribe Any YouTube Video in Real-Time
youtube.subtitle: Get a live, editable transcript of any YouTube video directly in Chrome — without copying, pasting, or switching tabs. Free mode included.
youtube.answer: LessonScriptor is a free Chrome extension that transcribes YouTube videos in real-time. Install it from the Chrome Web Store, open any YouTube video, click the LessonScriptor icon to open the side panel, and press play. The transcript appears immediately alongside the video — editable, highlightable, and exportable to Markdown or clipboard. It works on Coursera, Udemy, and any other Chrome tab, not just YouTube.

useCases.title: Who uses LessonScriptor to transcribe YouTube videos?

items.title: University students watching recorded lectures
items.desc: Many universities post lecture recordings on YouTube or link them in Moodle. LessonScriptor transcribes them in real-time, so students get searchable notes without rewatching.

items.title: Language learners studying with YouTube
items.desc: Watching videos in French, Spanish, or German? LessonScriptor transcribes in 14+ languages — the transcript helps with vocabulary and comprehension at the same time.

items.title: Online course students on Coursera and Udemy
items.desc: Coursera and Udemy videos play in Chrome just like YouTube. LessonScriptor works on both — giving you editable transcripts for every paid course you take.

methods.title: How to get a YouTube video transcript — all methods compared

items.cons: Read-only, no editing, not available on all videos, inaccurate on auto-generated captions

items.pros: Live editing, highlight, export, works on any video not just YouTube, 14+ languages, free mode

items.cons: No live transcription, not real-time, no editing, YouTube-only

steps.title: Step-by-step: transcribe a YouTube video with LessonScriptor
items: Install LessonScriptor from the Chrome Web Store (free, one click, no sign-up).
Open any YouTube video in Chrome.
Click the LessonScriptor icon in your Chrome toolbar to open the side panel.
Hit play on the video — transcription starts automatically.
Edit as you watch: click to add notes, highlight important passages, delete tangents.
Export to Markdown when done — your formatted notes are ready.

items.q: Can I download a transcript from a YouTube video?
items.a: Yes. With LessonScriptor, you can export the transcript as a Markdown file (.md) or copy it to clipboard to paste into Notion, Google Docs, or Obsidian. The export preserves all your formatting — bold, highlights, headers.

items.q: Is there a way to get a full transcript of a YouTube video?
items.a: Yes. YouTube's built-in transcript (click '...' → Show transcript) gives you a read-only, uneditable copy of auto-generated captions — not available for all videos. LessonScriptor generates a full editable transcript as the video plays, works on any video, and lets you export the complete text when done.

items.q: What's the difference between YouTube auto-captions and LessonScriptor?
items.a: YouTube auto-captions are read-only, appear as an overlay you can't edit, and aren't available on all videos. LessonScriptor transcribes in real-time into an editable side panel — you can correct errors, highlight passages, add notes, and export as Markdown. It also works on Coursera, Udemy, and any other video in Chrome, not just YouTube.

items.q: Does it work on YouTube videos without captions?
items.a: Yes. LessonScriptor doesn't rely on YouTube's captions — it transcribes the actual audio in real-time. So it works whether or not YouTube has auto-generated captions for the video.

items.q: Can I transcribe a YouTube video in French or Spanish?
items.a: Yes. LessonScriptor supports 14+ languages. In the extension settings, select your target language and it will transcribe accordingly — useful for language learning or international lectures.

items.q: Can I transcribe a private YouTube video?
items.a: Yes, if you have access to it. LessonScriptor transcribes any video playing in a Chrome tab — including YouTube unlisted or private videos you're logged in to view. It doesn't bypass any access restrictions; it just captures the audio from what's already playing on your screen.

items.q: Is there a limit to how long a YouTube video can be?
items.a: No hard limit. LessonScriptor transcribes in real-time as the video plays, so it works for any length — from a 5-minute tutorial to a 3-hour lecture.

youtube.metaTitle: Transcribe Any YouTube Video in Real-Time — Free Chrome Extension
youtube.metaDesc: Get a live, editable transcript of any YouTube video in Chrome. Free mode included. Works on Coursera, Udemy, and any video tab.

liveCaptions.h1: Live Captions for Chrome — Editable, Exportable, Multilingual
liveCaptions.subtitle: Add real-time, editable captions to any video you watch in Chrome. Free, private, and works on every platform — not just YouTube.
liveCaptions.answer: LessonScriptor adds live AI captions to any video in Chrome via a side panel. Unlike Chrome's built-in live captions, these are fully editable, exportable, and work with 14+ languages — including academic vocabulary your browser's captions get wrong.

vsChrome.title: LessonScriptor vs Chrome's built-in Live Captions

steps.title: How to add live captions in Chrome — step by step
items: Install LessonScriptor from the Chrome Web Store. It's free, takes 10 seconds, no sign-up required.
Open any video in Chrome — YouTube, Coursera, a Zoom recording, Google Meet, any platform.
Click the LessonScriptor icon in your Chrome toolbar to open the side panel next to the video.
Hit play on the video. Live captions appear in real-time inside the side panel automatically.
Click anywhere in the transcript to edit, correct a word, or add a note as you watch.
Use highlights to mark key moments — 5 colours, bold, headers to structure your notes.
Export when done — copy to clipboard or download as Markdown, ready for Notion, Obsidian, or Google Docs.

useCases.title: What you can use Chrome live captions for

items.title: University lectures
items.description: Follow along with recorded or live lectures without missing words. Edit the transcript in real-time, highlight key concepts, and export structured notes when the lecture ends.

items.title: YouTube educational videos
items.description: Transcribe any YouTube tutorial, course, or documentary as it plays. No need to pause and retype — just highlight what matters and skip the rest.

items.title: Zoom and Teams recordings
items.description: LessonScriptor works on any video that plays in a Chrome tab — including Zoom cloud recordings and Teams videos. Get captions even when no transcript was generated.

items.title: Language learning
items.description: Watch videos in a foreign language and get live captions in that language. Supports French, Spanish, German, Portuguese, Mandarin, and 9 more languages — useful for accent comprehension and vocabulary.

items.title: ADHD and accessibility
items.description: Reading captions while listening significantly improves comprehension and retention for students with ADHD, auditory processing difficulties, or those in noisy environments.

items.title: Online courses and webinars
items.description: Transcribe Coursera, edX, Udemy, or any live webinar in Chrome. Export your notes after the session with all your edits and highlights preserved.

items.q: How do I add live captions to Chrome?
items.a: Install LessonScriptor from the Chrome Web Store. Open any video, click the extension icon to open the side panel, and hit play. Live captions appear automatically in real-time.

items.q: Does Chrome have live captions built in?
items.a: Yes, Chrome has a basic live captions feature under Settings → Accessibility → Live Caption. However, it only supports English, produces a read-only feed you can't edit or export, and doesn't handle technical vocabulary well. LessonScriptor supports 14+ languages and gives you a fully editable, exportable transcript.

items.q: How do I turn on live captions in Chrome for free?
items.a: Two options: Chrome's built-in live captions are free but English-only and not editable (Settings → Accessibility → Live Caption). LessonScriptor also has a free mode — install from the Chrome Web Store and it works immediately with no account required. The free mode uses your device's microphone for transcription.

items.q: Do Chrome live captions work with headphones?
items.a: Chrome's built-in live captions do not work with headphones because they rely on the microphone. LessonScriptor's Premium mode uses tab audio capture — it grabs audio directly from the browser tab rather than through a microphone, so headphones work perfectly.

items.q: Can I get live captions in languages other than English in Chrome?
items.a: Chrome's built-in captions are English-only. LessonScriptor supports 14+ languages including French, Spanish, German, Portuguese, Mandarin, Italian, Dutch, Polish, and more. Select your language in the extension settings before starting transcription.

items.q: Why are Chrome's built-in live captions inaccurate?
items.a: Chrome's live captions use an older on-device speech recognition model optimised for general speech, not academic or technical vocabulary. LessonScriptor's Premium mode uses a more accurate AI model and supports a personal vocabulary dictionary — you can add names, terms, and abbreviations it should always get right.

items.q: Can I export Chrome live captions?
items.a: Chrome's built-in live captions cannot be exported or saved — the text disappears when the video stops. LessonScriptor saves your full transcript as you go and lets you export to Markdown, copy to clipboard, or keep it in the extension for later.

items.q: Do Chrome live captions work on Zoom recordings?
items.a: Chrome's built-in live captions work on any audio playing in the browser, including Zoom recordings. LessonScriptor also works on any video playing in a Chrome tab, with the added benefit of an editable, exportable transcript.

items.q: Are Chrome live captions private?
items.a: Chrome's built-in live captions process audio on-device — nothing leaves your computer. LessonScriptor's free mode also uses on-device speech recognition. The Premium mode sends audio to a cloud API for better accuracy, but no data is stored after transcription.

transcribeVideo.h1: YouTube Transcript Generator — Free, No Upload
transcribeVideo.subtitle: Generate a transcript from any YouTube video, lecture recording, or Zoom call in real-time. No file upload, no account, no subscription. Works directly in Chrome.
transcribeVideo.answer: The fastest YouTube transcript generator is LessonScriptor — a free Chrome extension that generates a transcript from any YouTube video in real-time as it plays. No file upload, no account, no subscription. It also works on Coursera, Udemy, Zoom recordings, and university portals like Moodle and Canvas. The free mode uses the Web Speech API (on-device, private); the Premium mode uses OpenAI Whisper for higher accuracy on accents, technical vocabulary, and 14+ languages.

useCases.title: What types of videos can you transcribe to text?

items.title: University lecture recordings
items.desc: Transcribe Moodle, Canvas, Panopto, or Blackboard lecture videos in real-time. No download required — if the video plays in Chrome, LessonScriptor captures it.

items.title: YouTube tutorials and courses
items.desc: Convert any YouTube video to text as it plays. Edit the transcript, highlight key points, and export to Notion, Obsidian, or Google Docs.

items.title: Zoom and Teams recordings
items.desc: Open a Zoom cloud recording or a Microsoft Teams video in Chrome and transcribe it directly. Works even if the original had no captions.

items.title: Local MP4 and video files
items.desc: Drag and drop an MP4 or MOV file into a Chrome tab. LessonScriptor transcribes local files the same way as online videos — no upload needed.

howItWorks.title: How to generate a YouTube transcript in Chrome
howItWorks.subtitle: No upload. No waiting. No subscription.

steps.title: Install LessonScriptor from the Chrome Web Store
steps.desc: Free, one click, no sign-up. It takes under 30 seconds and works on any Chrome browser.

steps.title: Open any video and press play
steps.desc: Go to YouTube, Coursera, a Zoom recording, or any video in Chrome. Click the LessonScriptor icon to open the side panel, then press play.

steps.title: Edit and export your transcript
steps.desc: The transcript appears in real-time. Click to edit, highlight key passages, add headers. Export to Markdown or copy to clipboard when done.

items.q: What is the best free YouTube transcript generator?
items.a: LessonScriptor is the best free YouTube transcript generator. Install the Chrome extension, open any YouTube video, click the icon to open the side panel, and press play — the transcript generates in real-time. Edit, highlight, and export as Markdown. Free mode is free forever with no sign-up.

items.q: Can I convert an MP4 to text?
items.a: Yes. Open the MP4 file in Chrome (drag and drop into a Chrome tab, or open via File → Open), then use LessonScriptor to transcribe it in real-time as it plays.

items.q: What is the most accurate YouTube transcript generator?
items.a: LessonScriptor's Premium mode is the most accurate YouTube transcript generator, using OpenAI Whisper (trained on 680,000 hours of speech). The free mode uses the Web Speech API — accurate for general content in clear audio. Both work without a subscription.

items.q: How long does it take to transcribe a video?
items.a: LessonScriptor transcribes in real-time — the text appears as the video plays, so there's no waiting. A 1-hour video takes 1 hour to transcribe.

items.q: Does LessonScriptor work offline?
items.a: The Free mode works offline — it uses your browser's built-in Web Speech API, which runs locally on your device. The Premium AI mode requires an internet connection to send audio to the cloud transcription engine.

items.q: What video platforms does LessonScriptor work on?
items.a: LessonScriptor works on any video that plays in a Chrome tab. That includes YouTube, Coursera, Udemy, Zoom cloud recordings, Microsoft Teams recordings, Moodle, Canvas, Panopto, Blackboard, and local MP4 or MOV files opened in Chrome.

items.q: What languages can I transcribe video into?
items.a: LessonScriptor supports 14+ languages including English, French, Spanish, German, Portuguese, Italian, Dutch, Polish, Mandarin, Japanese, Arabic, Hindi, and more. Select your language in the extension settings before pressing play.

otterAlt.h1: The Best Otter.ai Alternative for Students
otterAlt.subtitle: Looking for an Otter.ai alternative that works in your browser, doesn't require a subscription, and is free for everyday use? That's LessonScriptor.
otterAlt.answer: Otter.ai charges $17/month and caps free users at 300 minutes per month — enough for roughly 5 lectures. LessonScriptor is a Chrome extension with a free mode that has no monthly cap and a premium mode that costs ~€1/hour with no subscription. Unlike Otter.ai, which is designed for meetings and Zoom calls, LessonScriptor works on any video in Chrome: YouTube, Coursera, Udemy, university portals like Moodle or Canvas, and local video files.

whenToUse.title: When should you use Otter.ai vs LessonScriptor?

otter.heading: Use Otter.ai if you…
items: Need to transcribe live Zoom, Google Meet, or Microsoft Teams meetings
Want automatic meeting summaries and AI action items from calls
Work in a team that shares meeting notes through Otter's workspace
Primarily record your own voice — Otter's mobile app is strong for dictation

lessonscriptor.heading: Use LessonScriptor if you…
items: Need to transcribe video lectures on YouTube, Coursera, Udemy, or university portals
Want a free tool with no monthly minute cap
Prefer to pay per-use (~€1/hr) rather than a fixed monthly subscription
Need to transcribe in 14+ languages, not just English
Want to edit and highlight the transcript while the video plays

items.q: What is the best free alternative to Otter.ai?
items.a: LessonScriptor is the best free Otter.ai alternative for students. Its free mode is free forever with no monthly minute limit — it uses your microphone to capture audio from any video in Chrome. No subscription, no sign-up required.

items.q: Is there a completely free Otter.ai alternative with no limits?
items.a: Yes. LessonScriptor's free mode has no monthly minute cap — unlike Otter.ai's free tier, which is capped at 300 minutes per month. LessonScriptor uses your device's microphone and the Web Speech API, which runs locally with no usage limits.

items.q: Is there an Otter.ai alternative that works on YouTube?
items.a: Yes — LessonScriptor. Unlike Otter.ai, which is designed for meetings and calls, LessonScriptor works on any video in Chrome, including YouTube, Coursera, Udemy, Zoom recordings, and university portals.

items.q: Does Otter.ai work on YouTube?
items.a: No. Otter.ai is designed for live meetings — Zoom, Google Meet, Microsoft Teams, and in-person recordings via its mobile app. It does not transcribe YouTube videos, Coursera courses, or other browser-based video content. LessonScriptor works on all of those.

items.q: Which is more accurate: Otter.ai or LessonScriptor?
items.a: For meeting transcription, Otter.ai is purpose-built and performs well with clear speaker audio. For video-based content — YouTube, lectures, courses — LessonScriptor's Premium mode uses a high-accuracy AI model optimised for that context. LessonScriptor's free mode uses the Web Speech API, which is slightly less accurate than both in noisy environments.

items.q: Why is Otter.ai expensive for students?
items.a: Otter.ai's Pro plan starts at $17/month. For a student who only needs to transcribe lectures during the semester, that adds up to $100+ per year. LessonScriptor's premium mode costs about €1/hour with no subscription — a full semester of 12 hours costs around €12.

otterAlt.metaTitle: Best Otter.ai Alternative for Students — Free, No Subscription
otterAlt.metaDesc: LessonScriptor is the best free Otter.ai alternative for students. No monthly cap, works on any video in Chrome, pay-as-you-go premium.

privacy.whoWeAreBody: LessonScriptor is a Chrome extension developed and operated by Pierre Girardot. We are committed to protecting your privacy and respecting the European General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and other applicable privacy laws. For any privacy-related questions, contact us at contact@lessonscriptor.com.
privacy.whatWeCollectBody1: LessonScriptor is designed with privacy by default. We do not collect personal data about you. We do not create accounts for you, and we never ask for your email address. There is no sign-up — ever.
privacy.whatWeCollectMic: Microphone Mode: All audio processing happens locally on your device using the Web Speech API. Audio never leaves your device and is never sent to any server. Transcripts are stored only in your browser's local storage. We have no access to this data.
privacy.whatWeCollectTab: Tab Audio Mode: When you use Tab Audio mode, audio chunks from the browser tab are sent to Groq (api.groq.com) for transcription using their Whisper model. Groq does not retain audio data beyond the transcription request. Transcripts are saved locally in your browser only and are never transmitted to LessonScriptor's servers. We do not retain any personal data.
privacy.whatWeCollectNoTracking: We do not use cookies. We do not use analytics tools. We do not track your browsing, behavior, or usage patterns. We do not sell data.
privacy.localStorageBody: Your transcripts and extension settings are stored exclusively in your browser's local storage. This data lives on your device only. LessonScriptor has no server-side storage of transcripts, settings, or user information. If you clear your browser data, your local transcripts will be deleted. We cannot access or recover this data for you.
privacy.paymentsBody1: Tab Audio credits are purchased through LemonSqueezy, a third-party payment processor. Your payment details and personal information are handled entirely by LemonSqueezy — LessonScriptor never receives or stores your payment information, billing address, or email address.
privacy.paymentsBody2: Upon purchase, LemonSqueezy provides an anonymous license key. That key is stored locally on your device only. LessonScriptor has no way to associate your license key with your identity. Please review LemonSqueezy's privacy policy for details on how they handle payment data.
privacy.thirdPartyGroq: Groq (api.groq.com): Tab Audio mode uses Groq's Whisper model for high-accuracy transcription. Audio chunks are sent to Groq, processed, and not retained beyond the request. Please review Groq's privacy policy for details.
privacy.thirdPartyLemon: LemonSqueezy: Handles all payment processing for Tab Audio credit purchases. See the Payments section above.
privacy.thirdPartyGSC: Google Search Console: We use Google Search Console to monitor aggregate search performance of our website (lessonscriptor.com). This is limited to non-personally-identifiable search metrics and does not track individual users.
privacy.thirdPartyNoShare: We do not share your data with any other third parties.
privacy.gdprIntro: Under the GDPR, you have the following rights:
privacy.gdprRight1: Right to Access: You have the right to request a copy of the personal data we hold about you.
privacy.gdprRight2: Right to Rectification: You have the right to correct inaccurate personal data.
privacy.gdprRight3: Right to Erasure: You have the right to request the deletion of your personal data.
privacy.gdprRight4: Right to Data Portability: You have the right to request your personal data in a portable format.
privacy.gdprRight5: Right to Object: You have the right to object to our processing of your personal data.
privacy.gdprNote: Important note: Since LessonScriptor does not collect personal data on our servers, most of these rights are moot for data processed by us. Your transcripts exist only in your browser's local storage, which you control directly. Contact us at contact@lessonscriptor.com with any questions.
privacy.dataRetentionBody: LessonScriptor retains no personal data on our servers. Your transcripts and settings are retained only in your browser's local storage for as long as you choose to keep them. You can delete them at any time by clearing your browser data or uninstalling the extension.
privacy.changesBody: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices or legal requirements. We will notify you of any material changes by updating the last updated date on this page. Your continued use of LessonScriptor after such changes constitutes your acceptance of the updated policy.
privacy.contactBody: If you have any questions, concerns, or requests regarding this Privacy Policy or our privacy practices, please contact us at:
privacy.englishNote: This Privacy Policy is provided in English across all language versions of our website. English is the authoritative version.

hero.subtitle: Stop splitting your attention between listening and writing. LessonScriptor transcribes your lectures automatically so your working memory can focus on what matters: understanding.
hero.answer: LessonScriptor is an ADHD-friendly Chrome extension that auto-transcribes any lecture, YouTube video, or Coursera course in real time. ADHD students struggle because simultaneously listening and writing overwhelms working memory — LessonScriptor removes that split-attention problem entirely.

problems.title: Why note-taking is harder with ADHD
problems.subtitle: ADHD affects the exact cognitive systems that conventional note-taking demands most.

items.title: Working memory overload
items.description: ADHD reduces working memory capacity. Writing a sentence while processing the next one is cognitively impossible — one task always loses. Research shows ADHD students retain 40% less information when forced to write and listen simultaneously.

items.title: Split attention collapse
items.description: Dopamine-driven attention can't sustain two competing demands. The moment your pen hits the page, the lecturer's voice becomes background noise. LessonScriptor breaks that cycle by handling the writing task completely.

items.title: Information overload
items.description: Fast-paced lectures on Canvas, Coursera, or YouTube generate more information than ADHD working memory can buffer. Missing one sentence creates a comprehension gap that cascades — leaving students lost for the next five minutes.

features.title: How LessonScriptor helps ADHD students
features.subtitle: Three features designed specifically for how ADHD brains process information.

items.title: Automatic transcription
items.description: LessonScriptor transcribes every word your lecturer says — automatically, in real time, directly in Chrome. No setup, no switching tabs. Your hands stay free and your attention stays on understanding, not writing.

items.title: Real-time editing
items.description: The live transcript is fully editable. ADHD students can highlight key concepts, bold important terms, and add margin notes without losing the thread of the lecture. The editor works exactly like a document — no learning curve.

items.title: Timestamp navigation
items.description: Every transcript segment is timestamped. Jump back 30 seconds when dopamine pulled your attention away. Review the exact moment you zoned out, without rewatching the entire video. Works on YouTube, Coursera, Udemy, and any Chrome-based video.

quote.text: I used to fall behind the moment I picked up my pen. Now I just listen — and the transcript is there when I need it.
quote.attribution: LessonScriptor user, university student with ADHD

items.q: What are the best apps for students with ADHD?
items.a: The best apps for ADHD students remove tasks that compete with listening and understanding. LessonScriptor eliminates note-taking entirely by auto-transcribing lectures in Chrome. Other useful tools include focus timers (Pomofocus), text-to-speech readers (Natural Reader), and distraction blockers (Freedom). LessonScriptor is free to install with no account required.

items.q: How do I take notes with ADHD?
items.a: The most effective ADHD note-taking strategy is to stop writing during the lecture entirely. Let LessonScriptor transcribe automatically, then review and annotate the transcript immediately after — when working memory is no longer split. This approach is backed by dual-task research showing that eliminating the writing task dramatically improves comprehension and recall for ADHD learners.

items.q: Is LessonScriptor an ADHD-friendly app?
items.a: Yes. LessonScriptor was built for online students who struggle with simultaneous listening and writing — a core difficulty for people with ADHD. It works in Chrome on any video platform: YouTube, Coursera, Udemy, Canvas, Zoom recordings, and more. The free mode has no usage limits and requires no account.

items.q: What ADHD study tools work with online courses?
items.a: For online courses on platforms like Coursera, Udemy, or Canvas, LessonScriptor is the most effective ADHD study tool because it transcribes the video content directly in Chrome — no separate app, no copying and pasting. Other helpful tools include speed control (built into most platforms at 0.75x), closed captions (enabled via LessonScriptor), and the Cornell Notes method applied to the post-lecture transcript.

items.q: Does LessonScriptor work on YouTube for ADHD students?
items.a: Yes. LessonScriptor works on YouTube, Coursera, Udemy, university portals, Zoom recordings, and any video playing in Chrome. ADHD students can transcribe a YouTube lecture, pause to re-read any section, and export the full transcript as a Word document or PDF for later review — all without leaving the browser.

items.q: Is there a free ADHD note-taking tool for students?
items.a: LessonScriptor's free mode is completely free with no monthly minute limit. It uses the Web Speech API to transcribe audio from your microphone — capturing any video playing in Chrome. There is no sign-up, no subscription, and no credit card. Premium mode adds cloud AI accuracy for technical or accented speech, at around €0.60 per hour.

adhdStudents.metaTitle: LessonScriptor for ADHD Students — Stop Splitting Attention Between Listening and Writing
adhdStudents.metaDesc: LessonScriptor is the ADHD-friendly Chrome extension that auto-transcribes your lectures, freeing working memory for actual learning. No writing. No missed points.
